original_hypothesis.title: FeeCollectable allows setting _feeBips > 10000 due to validating old feeBips, causing underflow/reverts in calculateFees and potential permanent misconfiguration
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: FeeCollectable.setFeeParameters checks `if (feeBips > BIPS)` instead of validating the new input `_feeBips`. A permitted fee operator can set an excessive fee (including >100%), which can break downstream fee calculations (underflow in `userAmount = amountIn - feeAmount`) and can also brick further updates because subsequent calls revert when the stored feeBips is already > BIPS.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a confirmed code defect. Real-world exploitation depends on which production contracts inherit FeeCollectable and whether fee operators are compromised/malicious; still valuable because it can permanently wedge fee logic and potentially block core operations that call calculateFees.
deployment_assumptions: A production contract inherits FeeCollectable and uses calculateFees in state-changing paths
An account with isFeeOperator(msg.sender)==true calls setFeeParameters with _feeBips > 10000
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No inheriting production contract shown in provided scope; must identify inheritors in repo outside shown snippets or future additions.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setFeeParameters validates `feeBips > BIPS` (old state) not `_feeBips`; calculateFees subtract can underflow when feeBips > 10000

preconditions: Caller is an allowed fee operator (isFeeOperator returns true)
The contract has not already set feeBips > BIPS (first bad set can succeed)
Downstream code calls calculateFees(amountIn) and assumes feeAmount <= amountIn

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: validates wrong variable (state instead of input)
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: feeBips can exceed BIPS leading to underflow/reverts
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No input validation for _feeBips and no bounds on feeCollector

analysis_questions: Which contracts in the broader codebase inherit FeeCollectable and are deployed?
Do any critical user paths depend on calculateFees (swaps, withdrawals)?
Is there a recovery path if feeBips is set above BIPS (likely no, due to revert guard on subsequent calls)?
validation_requirements: Identify all inheritors and map calculateFees call sites
Simulate setting _feeBips=20000 and observe calculateFees revert behavior
Confirm subsequent setFeeParameters calls revert (permanent wedge)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Malicious or compromised fee operator sets _feeBips to >100% causing all fee-charging operations to revert (or miscompute), effectively freezing protocol operations or redirecting all value to feeCollector if logic clamps elsewhere.
prerequisites: A live inheriting contract that uses calculateFees for value-moving operations

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Protocol operation freeze or incorrect fee extraction depending on use site
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users of inheriting fee-charging modules

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing bounds tests for fee parameter updates and fee calculation under extreme feeBips

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: single tx for misconfiguration; persistent afterwards

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
